cells.cell_text: Time period for blood pressure assessment

page.content: The first table outlines various types of sensors and devices with their respective number, placement criteria, total consumption (mA), and comments: the opening sensor has 8 units, with a placement of 1/window 1/airflow door, requiring a KNX interface; the presence detector consists of 7 units, placed 1/room, with a total consumption of 56 mA and an installation height of 2.20 m; the motion detector includes 4 units, for 1/passing area, consuming 16 mA and featuring a bus coupler and connection terminal; the temperature sensor has 6 units, placed 1/room, with no specified consumption; the luminaires actuator comprises 12 units, depending on the luminaires number, with a total consumption of 75 mA and a binary input for dry contacts connection or conventional push button; the heating electrovalve has 6 units, placed 1/room, consuming 5.2 mA; and the split control interface features 4 units, placed 1/split, with no specified consumption and serving as a bidirectional KNX gateway for air conditioners. The second table presents blood pressure assessment data, indicating the diastolic BP (per 10 mmHg) has a baseline RR of 1.07 (0.94–1.21) and a Pvalue of 0.30, with a mean follow-up RR of 1.20 (0.97–1.49) and a Pvalue of 0.09. The systolic BP (per 10 mmHg) shows a baseline RR of 1.09 (1.01–1.17) with a Pvalue of 0.02 and a mean follow-up RR of 1.30 (1.15–1.48) with a Pvalue of <0.01. Finally, the pulse pressure (per 10 mmHg) has a baseline RR of 1.12 (1.02–1.23) and a Pvalue of 0.02, with a mean follow-up RR of 1.28 (1.11–1.47) and a Pvalue of <0.01.

lines.text: The first table outlines various types of sensors and devices with their respective number, placement criteria, total consumption
(mA), and comments: the opening sensor has 8 units, with a placement of 1/window 1/airflow door, requiring a KNX interface;
the presence detector consists of 7 units, placed 1/room, with a total consumption of 56 mA and an installation height of 2.20 m;
the motion detector includes 4 units, for 1/passing area, consuming 16 mA and featuring a bus coupler and connection terminal;
the temperature sensor has 6 units, placed 1/room, with no specified consumption; the luminaires actuator comprises 12 units,
depending on the luminaires number, with a total consumption of 75 mA and a binary input for dry contacts connection or
conventional push button; the heating electrovalve has 6 units, placed 1/room, consuming 5.2 mA; and the split control interface
features 4 units, placed 1/split, with no specified consumption and serving as a bidirectional KNX gateway for air conditioners.
The second table presents blood pressure assessment data, indicating the diastolic BP (per 10 mmHg) has a baseline RR of 1.07
(0.94–1.21) and a Pvalue of 0.30, with a mean follow-up RR of 1.20 (0.97–1.49) and a Pvalue of 0.09. The systolic BP (per 10
mmHg) shows a baseline RR of 1.09 (1.01–1.17) with a Pvalue of 0.02 and a mean follow-up RR of 1.30 (1.15–1.48) with a
Pvalue of <0.01. Finally, the pulse pressure (per 10 mmHg) has a baseline RR of 1.12 (1.02–1.23) and a Pvalue of 0.02, with a
mean follow-up RR of 1.28 (1.11–1.47) and a Pvalue of <0.01.
